name: Compose Networking Debug
description: Diagnose and fix networking issues between Docker Compose services including DNS resolution, port conflicts, and connectivity problems.

steps.title: List Compose networks
steps.description: See which networks the Compose stack created.

steps.title: Inspect a network to see connected containers
steps.description: View detailed info about a network including which containers are attached and their IP addresses.

steps.title: Test DNS resolution between services
steps.description: Verify that service discovery works by resolving a service name from within another container.
steps.command: docker compose exec api nslookup db
steps.tip: In Compose, services can reach each other by service name. If DNS fails, check that both services are on the same network.

steps.title: Test connectivity between services
steps.description: Verify that one service can actually reach another on the expected port.
steps.command: docker compose exec api sh -c 'nc -zv db 5432 || echo Connection failed'
steps.warning: If nc is not available, try: wget -qO- --spider http://db:5432 or install it with apk add netcat-openbsd.

steps.title: Debug with netshoot on the Compose network
steps.description: Attach a full networking toolkit to the Compose network for advanced debugging.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Compose Networking Debug
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Compose Networks ==="
docker network ls --filter 'label=com.docker.compose.project'

echo ""
echo "=== Service Connectivity ==="
for network in $(docker network ls --filter 'label=com.docker.compose.project' -q); do
  echo "Network: $(docker network inspect "$network" --format '{{.Name}}')"
  docker network inspect "$network" --format '{{range .Containers}}  {{.Name}}: {{.IPv4Address}}{{println}}{{end}}'
done

echo ""
echo "=== Debug Commands ==="
echo "DNS lookup:   docker compose exec <service> nslookup <target-service>"
echo "Port check:   docker compose exec <service> nc -zv <target-service> <port>"
echo "Netshoot:     docker run -it --rm --network <network-name> nicolaka/netshoot"
echo "Ping:         docker compose exec <service> ping -c 3 <target-service>"

faq.question: Why can't my service reach another service?
faq.answer: The most common causes are: services on different networks, target service not running, wrong port number, or firewall rules. Check 'docker compose ps' first to ensure all services are up.

faq.question: How do I expose a service to the host but not other services?
faq.answer: Use 'ports' to expose to the host and 'expose' for inter-service communication. Services on the same network can always reach each other on their internal ports.
